faqs.question: Where's the best place to stay on the Gold Coast with kids?
faqs.answer: For theme park access, base yourself in the Helensvale to Oxenford corridor—you'll cut travel time in half. If beaches are the priority, Broadwater Tourist Park and Ocean Beach Tourist Park put you right near patrolled surf. NRMA Treasure Island offers a middle ground with pools and activities on-site. Visit between April and October when the weather's more settled and those summer storms won't crash your beach days.

faqs.question: What's the best family accommodation on the Gold Coast with kids?
faqs.answer: Holiday parks tick all the boxes for families—space to spread out, playgrounds, pools and kitchens to save on dining out. BIG4 Gold Coast Holiday Park and NRMA Treasure Island Holiday Resort are consistently top-rated. Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park is brilliant for calmer water activities, while Broadwater Tourist Park suits families wanting patrolled beaches nearby. Most offer everything from powered sites to family cabins.

faqs.question: What's the best caravan park on the Gold Coast?
faqs.answer: BIG4 Gold Coast Holiday Park leads the pack with modern facilities and great reviews from families. Broadwater Tourist Park is hard to beat for location near calm swimming spots, while Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park offers that relaxed, nature-based vibe with creek access. Ocean Beach Tourist Park suits surfers and beach lovers. Each has its strengths—choose based on whether you're chasing theme parks, beaches or waterways.

faqs.question: What's the best caravan park on the Gold Coast for kids?
faqs.answer: NRMA Treasure Island Holiday Resort wins for keeping kids entertained with pools, jumping pillows and activities on-site. BIG4 Gold Coast Holiday Park is another standout with excellent facilities and happy campers. For something different, try Tallebudgera Creek Tourist Park where kids can kayak, paddleboard and spot wildlife right from the park. The shallow creek is perfect for little ones still building water confidence.

faqs.question: Where can I get good photos of kids on the Gold Coast?
faqs.answer: Head to Tallebudgera Creek at sunrise or sunset for magic-hour shots with calm water reflections. Currumbin Rock Pools offer lush rainforest backdrops without the crowds. The patrolled beaches near Broadwater Tourist Park have those classic Gold Coast surf-and-sand moments. For theme park shots, most parks allow cameras on rides. Natural Bridge in Springbrook National Park delivers stunning cave and waterfall photography, just thirty minutes from the coast.

faqs.question: What's Jericho on the Gold Coast for kids?
faqs.answer: This seems to reference a specific venue or program that isn't widely known across the Gold Coast family scene. The Gold Coast offers plenty of other proven options—theme parks like Dreamworld and Movie World, wildlife encounters at Currumbin Sanctuary, and dozens of playgrounds and water parks. If you're looking for something particular, the holiday parks like BIG4 and NRMA Treasure Island run their own kids' activities and entertainment programs.

faqs.question: Where do kids bowl free on the Gold Coast?
faqs.answer: Several Gold Coast bowling centres run kids bowl free promotions, typically during school holidays or off-peak times. Programs change seasonally, so ring ahead to places like Timezone or local bowling alleys before visiting. Rainy day backup plan: most holiday parks including NRMA Treasure Island and BIG4 Gold Coast have indoor games rooms. The theme parks and Currumbin Wildlife Sanctuary also offer all-weather entertainment if the skies open up.

faqs.question: What should we do on the Gold Coast with kids?
faqs.answer: The beauty of the Gold Coast is having everything close—theme parks, beaches and rainforest within thirty minutes. Hit Dreamworld or Movie World for thrills, spend mornings at patrolled beaches, then cool off at Tallebudgera Creek for calmer water. Currumbin Wildlife Sanctuary is brilliant for animal encounters. Time your visit between April and October for clearer skies and less humidity. Staying near Helensvale cuts theme park travel time significantly.

faqs.question: What are the best rainy day activities for kids on the Gold Coast?
faqs.answer: Head indoors to Timezone for arcade games and bowling, or visit Infinity Attraction for mind-bending optical illusions. The theme parks still operate in light rain with shorter queues. Currumbin Wildlife Sanctuary has covered areas and the lorikeet feeding continues rain or shine. Many holiday parks including NRMA Treasure Island have indoor pools and games rooms. Shopping centres like Pacific Fair offer cinemas and entertainment when the weather turns properly ugly.
